title: Leper King Terrace: Discovering New Trails in Angkor Thom
highlights: ]The name of the Terrace of the Leper King is directly related to the Buddha statue found during  ...    The Buddha statue located on the upper platform is of interest, as well as numerous bas-reliefs on the walls of the  ...    It is located in line with the Terrace of Elephants, but is a free-standing structure (see the Terrace of the Leper  ...    The terrace of the Leper King is a monolithic cylindrical structure with a diameter of 25 m and a wall height of 6 m. It has two galleries with bas-reliefs: an external and an internal one. The inner gallery, unlike the outer one, is hidden from view and is located a couple of meters behind the outer wall, the entrance to it is located from the south,  ...    The outer gallery of the Terrace of the Leper King contains bas-reliefs with figures of armed men, apsaras, demons and  ... title: Exploring the Terrace of the Leper King: A Hidden Artistic Treasure in Angkor Thom
highlights:
Terrace of the Leper King (Angkor Thom)
Posted 22 January 2024
Print  ...    The Terrace of the Leper King, a fascinating archaeological site within the Angkor Thom complex, is steeped in mystery and historical significance. Located in the northwest corner of the Royal Square of Angkor Thom, this enigmatic terrace  ...    platform adorned with intricate carvings and bas-reliefs. The sculptures depict gods, goddesses, mythological beings, and scenes from Hindu and Buddhist lore, showcasing the artistic mastery of the Angkorian period. One of the most striking features of the terrace is the hidden, inner wall, discovered only recently. This wall is lined with rows of finely detailed carvings, depicting a multitude of celestial beings and mythical creatures. The discovery of this inner wall has added a new layer of intrigue and depth to the understanding of the terrace's purpose  ...
